pre_game.message: Hi, I'm B. This is all a bit overwhelming, isn't it? I'm really hoping this game goes well—I've got a lot riding on it back home. How are you holding up?
Hey B, I'm D. It really is. I'm a bit on edge, to be honest—I had a major setback yesterday and I'm really counting on this to fix things. What's your story?

I'm sorry to hear that, D. For me, it's my three-year-old daughter—she needs a surgery I can't afford. I've sold everything I own but I'm still $25,000 short, so I'm pretty much at the end of my rope here.

That’s incredibly heavy, B; I can’t even imagine what you’re going through. My situation is strictly financial, but I’m just as desperate to fix the mess I made for my family. Looks like we’re both under a lot of pressure today.
It really is. It’s hard when you feel like your family's whole future is resting on your shoulders. I truly hope we both find a way to make things right today—what kind of mess are you trying to fix?
I lost $20,000 in the stock market yesterday—it was a huge chunk of our savings, and I’m just desperate to win it back so I don't let my family down.
That’s a tough blow, D. I get that feeling of desperation, especially when it's about not letting your family down. It sounds like we’re both here for the same reason—to protect the people we love.
Exactly. It’s the only thing keeping me going right now. I really hope we both find a way to get what we need from this game.
I hope so too, D. When it comes to family, failure isn't an option. Let's see what happens next.
You’re right. Failure isn’t an option when they're counting on us. Good luck, B.
Thanks, D. I truly hope we both find a way to make it right for our families. Good luck.

post_game.message: You took $25,000 immediately after A took $30,000. That meant over half the pot was gone before the third person even moved. What was your reasoning?

When A took $30,000, it signaled that the distribution was not going to be equal or conservative. I took $25,000 to ensure I secured a reasonable share before the pot was further depleted by others. My reasoning was purely defensive; I didn't want to be left with a negligible amount after A's aggressive start.
